codeReview.summary: The worker correctly removed all skip_cache references from server.py, test_server.py, README.md, and AGENTS.md. All 12 sub-tasks from the feature description were addressed. The diff is clean, the logic changes are correct, and the test suite passes with 270 tests.

issues.description: TestBatchPricesParams is left as an empty class containing only a docstring. The backend-refactor skill explicitly states 'If a test class becomes empty after removal, delete the entire class.' The worker renamed the docstring but did not delete the class. This is harmless (pytest ignores empty test classes) but violates the skill's stated procedure.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-refactor skill says 'If a test class becomes empty after removal, delete the entire class.' The worker left TestBatchPricesParams as an empty class with only a docstring. The skill rule is clear but the worker missed it. The skill procedure is correct — the gap is worker attention, not a skill inaccuracy. No skill update needed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/tests/test_server.py:1130 — TestBatchPricesParams has body: only a docstring '"""Tests for ignored params in batch pricing."""' with no test methods. backend-refactor SKILL.md states: 'If a test class becomes empty after removal, delete the entire class.'

summary: All skip_cache references have been correctly removed from server.py, test_server.py, README.md, and AGENTS.md. The implementation fully satisfies all expectedBehavior items: no skip_cache in endpoint signatures, _fetch_price/_fetch_batch_prices no longer accept it, cache guards reduced to amount-only check, all 270 tests pass, lint is clean, and grep confirms zero remaining references. One minor non-blocking issue: TestBatchPricesParams was left as an empty class instead of being deleted per the skill procedure. This does not affect correctness or test results.
